 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","title":"VFX perf regression guard + CI evidence logs","description":"Background
- We need a regression guard so VFX perf gains remain stable and detectable in CI.
- The guard should be deterministic, hash-verified, and report clear diagnostics.

Goals
- Add a VFX perf regression gate using the harness + timing logs.
- Fail fast on significant regressions with actionable JSONL diagnostics.

Scope / Work Items
- Define baseline thresholds per effect/size from bd-3e1t.5.7 (p50/p95/p99 total_ms).
- Add an automated check (test or E2E script) that runs the harness at fixed seed/time and compares metrics against thresholds.
- Validate JSONL schema and include first-mismatch diagnostics (effect, size, frame_idx, expected vs actual hash, timing deltas).
- Ensure the guard is deterministic and does not introduce runtime overhead when disabled.

Constraints
- No visual quality or resolution changes.
- Use existing harness/logging; avoid new files unless required by test infra.

Tests / Validation
- Guard fails on synthetic regression and passes on baseline.
- JSONL logs are schema-stable and include perf + hash fields.

Dependencies
- Requires timing instrumentation (bd-3e1t.5.2), golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.5), and baseline pack (bd-3e1t.5.7).","acceptance_criteria":"1) Automated guard compares p50/p95/p99 against baseline thresholds for defined effects and sizes.\n2) Guard fails with actionable diagnostics (effect, size, frame_idx, expected vs actual hash, timing deltas).\n3) JSONL schema validation passes and hashes remain stable for baseline runs.","notes":"Added PTY perf regression guard in crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/visual_effects_pty.rs. Emits JSONL event vfx_perf_guard with actuals + budgets + deltas. Release-only (skips debug). Added synthetic regression unit test vfx_perf_budget_rejects_regression to validate budget logic. Hash guard: loads golden checksums if present and compares prefix, logging vfx_perf_guard_hash (outcome pass/fail/missing) and mismatch diagnostics; missing goldens are logged but do not fail.  {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","title":"Render-thread profiling + in-app timing for VFX","description":"Background
- We need precise, low-noise timing data for Visual Effects to drive real optimization.
- Flamegraphs alone don’t connect hotspots to frame phases; we need structured, in-app timing.

Goals
- Measure per-frame time spent in update, render, diff, and present for VFX runs.
- Keep instrumentation deterministic and behind a flag so normal demos remain clean.
- Produce JSONL logs that correlate with the VFX harness and golden hashes.

Scope / Work Items
- Add a perf timing toggle (env/CLI) used by the VFX harness.
- Instrument the VFX update/render pipeline with lightweight timers (no allocations in hot path).
- Emit per-frame JSONL events with: run_id, effect, size, tick_ms, frame_idx, update_ms, render_ms, diff_ms, present_ms, total_ms.
- Emit a summary event at end of run: count, p50/p95/p99 for total_ms and top phases.
- Ensure timers are monotonic and stable (avoid wall clock; prefer std::time::Instant).

Constraints
- No visual output changes, no quality/resolution reductions.
- When perf toggle is OFF, no extra logging or measurable overhead on hot path.
- Deterministic: log values must match frame order and inputs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit test for the summary aggregator (p50/p95/p99 on a fixed dataset).
- E2E test: run harness with perf enabled and validate JSONL schema + required fields.
- Ensure missing fields or non-monotonic frame_idx fails fast with clear errors.

Notes
- This bead is a prerequisite for accurate hotspot selection in bd-3e1t.5.3.","acceptance_criteria":"1) With perf flag enabled, JSONL logs include per-frame timing fields and a summary record with p50/p95/p99.
2) With perf flag disabled, timing logs are absent and demo output is unchanged.
3) The VFX harness can consume and validate these logs in an automated E2E run.
4) Summary aggregator is unit-tested and produces deterministic results.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:07.537077007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T23:10:20.288638162Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T23:10:20.288617663Z","close_reason":"Implemented VFX perf timing JSONL + summary + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:07.537077007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:13.104642394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","title":"VFX hotspot matrix + algorithmic optimization pass","description":"Background
- We need dramatic VFX performance gains without any visual changes.
- Optimization must be data-driven: measure → pick hotspot → change one lever → prove identical output.

Goals
- Use profiling data to target the top VFX hotspots and reduce frame time substantially.
- Maintain strict isomorphism: identical inputs produce identical outputs (hashes must match).

Scope / Work Items
- Build a hotspot/opportunity matrix using perf logs + flamegraph:
  - List top 5 hotspots (function + line).
  - For each: Impact (1-5), Confidence (1-5), Effort (1-5), Score = (Impact×Confidence)/Effort.
- Select only Score ≥ 2.0 items for implementation.
- Apply a single, isolated optimization per change (cache, reuse, algorithmic improvement, etc.).
- After each change: reprofile and verify hashes with the golden registry.
- Document an isomorphism proof per change (ordering, tie-breakers, RNG/time).

Constraints
- No quality/resolution degradation, no visual output changes.
- Deterministic output required; hashes must be unchanged unless explicitly blessed.
- Avoid adding new files unless absolutely necessary; prefer existing logs/tests.

Tests / Validation
- Use harness + golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.1 + bd-3e1t.5.5).
- E2E run must report p50/p95/p99 improvement and matching hashes.
- If hashes differ, provide a deterministic root-cause analysis before any bless.

Success Target (Performance)
- Achieve a clearly measurable improvement: ≥30% reduction in p95 total frame time for at least two heavy effects (e.g., plasma/metaballs/doom/quake) under the harness.

Notes
- Always reprofile after each optimization; hotspots will shift.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Hotspot/opportunity matrix captured for top 5 VFX hotspots with scores.
2) At least one Score ≥ 2.0 optimization implemented with a single-lever change.
3) Harness shows ≥30% p95 total frame time reduction for at least two heavy effects.
4) Golden hashes remain identical (or are explicitly blessed with documented rationale).
5) Isomorphism proof recorded for each change.","notes":"Perf data (VFX harness, release, 120x40 @16ms):

+{"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","title":"VFX hotspot matrix + algorithmic optimization pass","description":"Background
- We need dramatic VFX performance gains without any visual changes.
- Optimization must be data-driven: measure → pick hotspot → change one lever → prove identical output.

Goals
- Use profiling data to target the top VFX hotspots and reduce frame time substantially.
- Maintain strict isomorphism: identical inputs produce identical outputs (hashes must match).

Scope / Work Items
- Build a hotspot/opportunity matrix using perf logs + flamegraph:
  - List top 5 hotspots (function + line).
  - For each: Impact (1-5), Confidence (1-5), Effort (1-5), Score = (Impact×Confidence)/Effort.
- Select only Score ≥ 2.0 items for implementation.
- Apply a single, isolated optimization per change (cache, reuse, algorithmic improvement, etc.).
- After each change: reprofile and verify hashes with the golden registry.
- Document an isomorphism proof per change (ordering, tie-breakers, RNG/time).

Constraints
- No quality/resolution degradation, no visual output changes.
- Deterministic output required; hashes must be unchanged unless explicitly blessed.
- Avoid adding new files unless absolutely necessary; prefer existing logs/tests.

Tests / Validation
- Use harness + golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.1 + bd-3e1t.5.5).
- E2E run must report p50/p95/p99 improvement and matching hashes.
- If hashes differ, provide a deterministic root-cause analysis before any bless.

Success Target (Performance)
- Achieve a clearly measurable improvement: ≥30% reduction in p95 total frame time for at least two heavy effects (e.g., plasma/metaballs/doom/quake) under the harness.

Notes
- Always reprofile after each optimization; hotspots will shift.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Hotspot/opportunity matrix captured for top 5 VFX hotspots with scores.
2) At least one Score ≥ 2.0 optimization implemented with a single-lever change.

4) Golden hashes remain identical (or are explicitly blessed with documented rationale).

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.4","title":"Startup perf: lazy-init heavy demo screens","description":"Background
- Demo startup time is inflated by eager initialization of all screens and effect state.
- For reliable perf measurements (and better UX), we should lazily initialize heavy screens.

Goals
- Reduce startup overhead by deferring heavy screen state until first access.
- Keep behavior identical once a screen has been visited.

Scope / Work Items
- Identify heavy screens (Visual Effects, File Browser, Graph/Diagram demos, etc.).
- Convert ScreenStates to lazily initialized Option/OnceCell-like fields.
- Ensure initialization happens exactly once per screen and is logged with duration.
- Provide deterministic behavior: same screen produces same output after initialization.
- Add a quick log event on screen init: screen_id, init_ms, effect_count, memory_estimate (if available).

Constraints
- No output changes or quality reductions.
- No new files unless absolutely necessary.
- Keep per-screen init cheap and avoid any work when screen is not visited.

Tests / Validation
- Unit test: screen registry can resolve an uninitialized screen and initialize on first use.
- E2E run: start app, navigate to each heavy screen, and assert init logs exist once per screen.
- Ensure no regression in existing snapshot tests for screen output.

Notes
- This is a prerequisite for cleaner profiling and better perceived performance.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Startup path avoids constructing heavy screen states (verified by init logs).
2) Each heavy screen initializes once on first access, with deterministic output thereafter.
3) Init log event emitted with screen_id + init_ms.
4) E2E navigation test verifies single init and unchanged snapshots.","notes":"Lazy-init for VisualEffects/CodeExplorer/FileBrowser/SnapshotPlayer; init JSONL logging + in-memory test events; added tests for first-use + single-init logging; ran cargo fmt/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:41.037173138Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:17:55.227340839Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:17:55.227293650Z","close_reason":"Implemented lazy init + init logging/tests; fmt/check/clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:41.037173138Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:46.526253368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","title":"VFX determinism: golden hash registry + frame suite","description":"Background
- Performance optimizations require ironclad evidence that outputs are unchanged.
- Current VFX tests do not lock a canonical per-frame hash suite for each effect.

Goals
- Establish a golden hash registry for VFX effects, covering a deterministic frame suite.
- Make hash verification a standard gate for performance work.

Scope / Work Items
- Define a deterministic frame suite per effect (e.g., frames 0..5) using fixed seed/time/size.
- Capture per-frame hashes and store them via existing test infra (insta snapshots or existing hash registry).
- Extend e2e harness to validate hash sequences and report first mismatch clearly.
- Add a bless workflow for intentional changes with explicit rationale.

Constraints
- No quality/resolution reductions.
- Deterministic environment: fixed size, fixed tick cadence, fixed seed/time.
- Avoid new files unless required by snapshot tooling.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests (ftui-demo-showcase) for hash sequences per effect.
- E2E run verifies all hashes and logs effect, frame_idx, hash, seed/time.
- Any mismatch fails fast with expected vs actual hash in logs.

Dependencies

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.2","title":"Demo: Determinism Lab (Checksum Equivalence)","description":"Background
- Correctness and determinism are core differentiators, but currently implicit.
- A dedicated lab can make the proof visible and visceral.

Goals
- Render identical scenes through multiple diff strategies and show checksum equivalence.
- Display per-frame change counts and mismatch warnings.
- Allow toggling strategy (Full, DirtyRows, Redraw) and seed.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Determinism".
- Indexed in Screen Registry with palette tags: "checksum", "diff", "proof".

Implementation Notes
- Use TerminalModel checksum or render-trace checksum logic for validation.
- Visualize mismatch as red banner with first differing coordinate and delta count.
- Include a mini timeline of checksum history for the last N frames.
- Deterministic with fixed seed and frozen time.

Acceptance Criteria
- Checksums match across strategies for identical inputs.
- Mismatch path shows first differing coordinate and delta count.
- User can export a verification report to JSONL.
- UI remains responsive under rapid toggling.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for checksum equivalence across strategies and seeds.
- Snapshot tests for match and mismatch UI states.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`):
